title: Run Internal – Streaming & Session Persistence

classes.name: OpenAIServerConversationTracker
classes.purpose: Tracks server-side conversation state for conversation-aware runs using three complementary dedupe views: object identity, stable server IDs, and content fingerprints.

classes.name: SingleStepResult
classes.purpose: Result of a single agent step; consumed by streaming and session helpers.

classes.name: RunResultStreaming
classes.purpose: Streaming run result; holds the async queue/task for guardrails and exposes stream_events().

classes.name: Session
classes.purpose: Abstract session store interface for persisting conversation items.

classes.name: RunState
classes.purpose: Mutable run state passed through the pipeline; tracks persisted item counts.

streaming.py: stream_step_result_to_queue(step_result: SingleStepResult, queue) -> None  # delegates to stream_step_items_to_queue(step_result.new_step_items)
session_persistence.py: prepare_input_with_session(input, session, session_input_callback, session_settings, *, include_history_in_prepared_input, preserve_dropped_new_items) -> tuple[prepared_input, items_to_persist]
persist_session_items_for_guardrail_trip(session, server_conversation_tracker, session_input_items, original_user_input, run_state, store) -> list | None
save_result_to_session(session, original_input, new_items, run_state, *, response_id, reasoning_item_id_policy, store) -> int
save_resumed_turn_items(*, session, items, persisted_count, response_id, reasoning_item_id_policy, store) -> int
guardrails.py: run_input_guardrails_with_queue(agent, guardrails, input, context, streamed_result, parent_span) -> None

key_relationships: stream_step_items_to_queue emits RunItemStreamEvent per RunItem subtype into asyncio.Queue
persist_session_items_for_guardrail_trip calls save_result_to_session; skips if OpenAIServerConversationTracker present
rewind_session_items calls Session.pop_item() and uses OpenAIServerConversationTracker.server_item_ids to strip strays
OpenAIServerConversationTracker.hydrate_from_state seeds tracking from RunState on resume
resolve_run_grouping reads Session.session_id; returns (kind, id) tuple with priority: conversation > session > group > run

session_persistence_logic.dedup_strategy: Track already_persisted count in RunState to skip re-persisting on retry; fingerprint-based dedup for OpenAIConversationsSession
session_persistence_logic.compaction: After save, if session is OpenAIResponsesCompactionAware and response has no local tool outputs, call session.run_compaction(); defer if local tools present
session_persistence_logic.guardrail_trip: On input guardrail tripwire, persist only the input items (not model output) before raising
session_persistence_logic.rewind: On conversation retry, pop_item() until all appended items removed; verify cleanup via wait_for_session_cleanup

guardrail_concurrency.streaming: run_input_guardrails_with_queue: asyncio.create_task per guardrail, asyncio.as_completed, cancels remaining on first tripwire; pushes results to streamed_result._input_guardrail_queue
guardrail_concurrency.non_streaming: run_input_guardrails: same pattern but raises InputGuardrailTripwireTriggered directly
guardrail_concurrency.output: run_output_guardrails: parallel tasks, raises OutputGuardrailTripwireTriggered on tripwire
guardrail_concurrency.tracing: Each guardrail runs inside guardrail_span(); tripwires attach SpanError to parent_span or current span

important_notes: ToolApprovalItem is intentionally NOT streamed; it signals an interruption surfaced via RunResultStreaming.interruptions
handoff_occured is intentionally misspelled for backward compatibility
OpenAIConversationsSession uses ID-agnostic fingerprinting (_ignore_ids_for_matching=True) and sanitizes provider fields before persistence
RunGrouping priority hierarchy: conversation_id > session.session_id > group_id > uuid4().hex (per-run)
Session.add_items is the only method called to persist; compaction is separate via run_compaction()
prepare_input_with_session uses deep-copy + reference maps to correctly separate 'new turn items' from reused history when a session_input_callback reorders/filters
